source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

actions_taken: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) manually activated by control room operator
All 87 personnel accounted for within four minutes
Level bridle drain valves closed and inspected for damage
event.summary: On 15 July 2020 at 1411 hours, the Appomattox platform (MC 437 A) experienced an unplanned gas release while attempting to start up Bulk Gas Compressor #1. Multiple gas detectors alarmed at 2:19 pm, triggering an automatic Process Shut Down. A deck operator detected the odor of gas and the control room operator manually activated the Emergency Shut Down and Muster Alarm. Investigation revealed that level bridle drain valves on the BGC #1 Gas Scrubber had been left open. Approximately 1838 kg of gas was released over a 25-minute period. No injuries or damages occurred. The facility safety system responded in accordance with applicable regulations.
key_phrases: level bridle drain valves left open
personnel failed to isolate LSHL bridle drain valves
personnel failed to perform thorough precheck walk-through
human performance error - rushing to get job completed
human performance error - not following proper procedures
human performance error - inattention to task

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in process system

threats.name: Open drain valves on level bridle during compressor startup
Inadequate precheck procedures before equipment startup

controls.name: Level bridle drain valve isolation procedure
controls.barrier_role: Prevent gas release by ensuring drain valves are closed before startup

supporting_text: Personnel failed to isolate the LSHL bridle drain valves before attempting to start up the BGC #1 compressor
The level bridle drain valves were left open on the BGC #1 Gas Scrubber
The valves have to be manually opened and closed

controls.name: Precheck walk-through of equipment before startup
controls.barrier_role: Identify open valves and other issues before equipment startup

supporting_text: Personnel failed to perform a thorough precheck walk-through of the equipment before putting it in service
Personnel failed to perform an adequate walk through of the BGC #1 Gas Compressor system prior to attempting to start it

controls.name: Gas detection system
controls.barrier_role: Detect gas release and alert personnel

supporting_text: At 2:19 pm, multiple gas detectors alarmed and a Process Shut Down (PSD) was automatically initiated
It is determined that the gas detectors functioned as designed
They alerted the CRO of a gas released and even though the detectors were acknowledged in the control room, they continued monitoring the release until the critical trip set point initiated the PSD

controls.name: Process Shut Down (PSD) system
controls.barrier_role: Automatically shut down process when gas is detected

supporting_text: At 2:19 pm, multiple gas detectors alarmed and a Process Shut Down (PSD) was automatically initiated
Shell's gas detection and safety shutdown system functioned per BSEE approved regulatory drawings

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system and Muster Alarm
controls.barrier_role: Manual emergency shutdown and personnel muster to prevent injuries

supporting_text: A deck operator in the area between the Utility and Process Module verified that he smelled gas and the control room operator manually activated the Emergency Shut Down (ESD) and the primary Muster Alarm
All 87 Personnel On Board were accounted for within four minutes
The CRO also acted appropriately by functioning the ESD and mustering personnel before the source of the release was known

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
